article_title: OpenAI, AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft and NVIDIA Release MRC 1.0 to OCP, an Open RDMA Protocol Already Running OpenAI's Largest Training Clusters

content_preview.title: OpenAI, AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft and NVIDIA Release MRC 1.0 to OCP, an Open RDMA Protocol Already Running OpenAI's Largest Training Clusters
content_preview.summary: Six-company collaboration publishes Multipath Reliable Connection 1.0 through the Open Compute Project on May 6 after production use in OpenAI's Blackwell-class supercomputers at Microsoft Fairwater and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Abilene.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A six-company collaboration of OpenAI, AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft and NVIDIA published version 1.0 of the Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) specification through the Open Compute Project on May 6, 2026, [according to NVIDIA](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/spectrum-x-ethernet-mrc/). The protocol, an RDMA transport for AI training fabrics, is already running OpenAI's largest Blackwell-class clusters at Microsoft's Fairwater and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's Abilene data centers,...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight 675-word News piece with proper hedging on 'What We Don't Know' (UEC roadmap, performance numbers, vendor hardware timelines).
editor_notes.source_verification: Read both snapshots from disk. NVIDIA blog (source-0): MRC, Multipath Reliable Connection, OCP release, Sachin Katti as 'head of industrial compute' at OpenAI, Blackwell deployment, Fairwater + Abilene + Oracle Cloud, ConnectX SuperNICs, Spectrum-X, Adaptive RDMA, AMD/Broadcom/Intel/Microsoft collaborator list, 'replacing a single-lane road...' verbatim, 'high levels of GPU utilization by load-balancing traffic across all available paths' verbatim, 'in just microseconds — detect a network path failure and reroute traffic automatically' verbatim, 'rely on MRC to deliver on performance, scale and efficiency requirements' verbatim, 'open specification' framing, May 6 announcement. OpenAI page (source-1): GB200 deployment language verified verbatim, Fairwater/Abilene confirmed, AMD/Broadcom/Intel/Microsoft collaborator list confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every direct quote and proper-noun specific traces to its cited source.
editor_notes.originality: First-of-cluster review for MRC/OCP in this batch (PRs #1195 and #1201 cover the same announcement and will be rejected as duplicates downstream).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. Two-source-but-tightly-coupled (vendor + customer announcement on same release) coverage of a multi-vendor specification handover.
